As Hett, he was able to teach the fighting style of [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Taras Kasi]].{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ===Force Powers=== {{Quote|I grew in understanding and power and made myself into Darth Krayt.|Darth Krayt to Cade Skywalker|}} As the Dark Lord of the One Sith, Darth Krayt was the strongest Sith Lord of his time. and one of the most capable that ever lived. In 40 ABY, the Jedi Grandmaster Luke was troubled with [[Force vision/Legends|visions]] of Krayt as a shadowy figure who seemed at least as ruthless and strong in the Force as the [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]], seeming like a dark spirit on a mission of doom.''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' The Dark Lord was powerful enough in the Force to battle a being as powerful as [[Abeloth]], though he was assisted by Grand Master Luke Skywalker, who himself was wary of dueling Krayt in beyond shadows, even to the point of almost backstabbing the Sith preemptively. Jacen Solo believed that if Krayt acquired Allana Solo as an apprentice that Krayt would become unstoppable and usher in an appalling new future, this is greatly what inspired him to try to conquer the galaxy as Darth Caedus. Put another way, Krayt was Luke Skywalker's dark counterpart in the contest for the Throne of Balance. ''The Force itself'' was bent to Krayt's will.[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] The darkness brought on by Darth Krayt's rise made it harder than ever for Jedi masters to use the Force to sense the future. Krayt stated that he had fully mastered the Jedi and Sith arts. Merely being in the same room as Darth Krayt was mentally painful for Cade Skywalker. All of ''this'' power was at Darth Krayt's command even while he was held back by the infestation of the Vong parasites and the wounds Kenobi dealt him. Krayt's strength in the dark side was great enough to be felt by every Sith in the galaxy. He claimed that after his resurrection, his power in the Force multiplied, indicating that since his defeat on Had Abbadon, he had grown considerably stronger than before. The dark side of the Force was said to be as though it lived through him, claims similar to those the Son on Mortis had made almost 200 years ago.{{TCW|Overlords}} To live on Krayt's throneworld was to forfeit one's soul and his power was great indeed to those who sensed it. His strength in the dark side was such that he could fight and overpower Cade Skywalker, who was armed with a lightsaber, with mostly his usage of the Force alone. Even before turning to the dark side, this man was noted as a powerful Jedi with great potential, such as by his father. As a Jedi Master, he was considered a worthy and powerful Jedi, such that he was one of the generals sent to seek to end the uprising of the CIS by aiding in the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|Outer Rim Sieges]]. Obi-Wan Kenobi at first assumed that Hett (not yet a Sith) must be one of that dark order due to his sheer power when the protector of Luke sensed the rampage of Hett across Tatooine in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' Krayt had also mastered the knowledge of Xoxaan. In addition to being a formidable, sometimes [[Force Enrage|enraged]] lightsaber duelist, as both as Hett and as Darth Krayt, he was also talented in the other applications of the Force, notably moving things with his will, Force lightning, communication, and healing. A'Sharad Hett possessed incredible skill in [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], as shown when able to non-lethally repulse a mob of his fellow Tuskans who were harassing Mundi, a feat that impressed the Jedi Master, though was seen as possibly too violent for the situation. When his company was attacked by Laninks, Hett threw two of them out of a speeder.[[Star Wars (1998) 15|''Star Wars'' (1998) 15]] During their duel, Hett and Sing exchanged Force strikes, slamming each other into walls as they dueled on a [[Unidentified jungle world (Kamdon system)|jungle planet]] in 30 BBY. He was able to [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|nearly kill]] Obi-Wan with a saber throw. Moreover, Kenobi did not breach his defenses with the Force until Hett had had his hand [[Cho sun/Legends|severed]] by Kenobi. During his time as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], Hett destroyed a sealed wall in order to heed the call of Xoxaan's holocron.[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]] As a Dark Lord in 44 ABY he showed continued mastery of this skill, such as [[Liberation of Coruscant (Lost Tribe of Sith emergence)|when]] he disarmed a Jedi, then later yanked Abeloth back to himself and Luke via recalling his embedded severed hand and thus effectively countering her [[Fold space|teleportation]], even as the attack caused her very essence to leak from her wounds. Late in the battle, after being severely wounded and mutilated, Krayt floated in the air above Luke. According to [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]], the power of self-levitation was impressive,[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] and Luke Skywalker previously believed such was impossible for a Jedi outside of low-gravity.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Vortex]]''''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]'' Nearly a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] later, Krayt threw Maladi to the ground without expressing significant effort, made officials kneel before him, disciplined Darth Nihl in the Valley of the Dark Lords by briefly Force choking him, and he redirected multiple stones thrown at him by Darth Wyyrlok after holding the barrage in [[Force Stasis/Legends|stasis]]. Once Krayt released his hold on the rocks as he flung them back, the traitor was forced to throw up a bubble of Force to avoid death. Krayt could slam his foes with the Force, both in bursts to strike many foes and as Force Pushes to throw Jedi around. Krayt subjected Cade Skywalker to a Force Strike to the head, chaining in Force attacks with saber blows with ease, befitting the mastery of Niman.[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] While more renowned for the grandeur and force of his telekinetic might, Krayt was also capable of incredible precision and delicacy with the Force, such as when burdened by the Yuuzhan Vong armor he crafted his [[Holocron of Darth Krayt|own Holocron]] (which held may secrets and plans only Krayt was privy to), a process that required a Sith to make many microscopic tweaks with the Force while being in tune with the will or currents of the Force.''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' Krayt was able to employ his powers for more mundane utility, such as moving machines or unlocking locks. During his duel with Cade Skywalker in 138 ABY, his strength in telekinesis was such that he could practically vaporize some of the stone pillars of the Sith Temple during his final duel with Cade Skywalker. Krayt flung the Jedi around in this fight without showing much exertion, slamming Skywalker into things and dragging him across and through a wall. It was believed that there would be no limit to what could be done with these telekinetic techniques, given enough power and wisdom. The raw power of his [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] was enough to crumble stone columns in the Sith Temple and kill multiple rakghouls with a single burst. During an [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempt against him, he demonstrated the power to surround his hand with lightning while fighting in melee. When faced with Celeste Morne's lightning, he was able to counter with his own, then began to overwhelm her. Indeed, even when she joined with Karness Muur's spirit, (Muur being such a master of the dark that Vader considered to be about as strong as Darth Sidious was in 19 BBY) she was unable to harm Krayt until he was backstabbed by Azlyn Rae, breaking his focus and leaving him with a hole in his body from her saber. Krayt could use his powers to create a shield/barrier with Force lightning to defend himself from those who threatened him. While burdened by his Vong armor, Darth Krayt used Sith Lightning to unlock the holocrons of Darth Bane, Darth Andeddu, and Darth Nihilus at once. When they refused to give any sort of useful advice and then attacked him, he closed them down with more lightning. Krayt was capable of great precision and control with his lightning, as shown when he traced Darth Talon's body with dark side electricity without hurting or striking her. Given the difficulity to control lighting save for their intensity its testament of his skills . His prowess with Force lightning exceeded that of Darth Wyyrlok, evidenced when Krayt blocked the barrage with his bare hands, before Krayt chose to take control of the situation and throw aside the traitor's lightning, destroying the terrain around them. Luke Skywalker was wary of being hit by his lightning. Moreover, before even becoming a Sith, Hett could slaughter squads of armored Yuuzhan Vong soldiers with blasts of lightning, and also may have known how to [[Pyrokinesis|conjure fire]] against the Vong, though this may have been merely narrative flair in how he spoke of the battle. [[File:ASharadHettvsYuuzhanVong-Legacy2006-18.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Hett using Force lightning to kill his torturers.]] A'Sharad Hett could [[Enhance Attribute|augment his physical capabilities]], such as when he used his Jedi powers to be able to destroy droids with a [[Force punch|single punch]]. Hett could leap from a speeder that crashed through a window, then, unharmed, attack the terrorists around him. Jedi of his time were expected to learn how to [[Tutaminis|absorb energy]] and as a Sith he demonstrated even better skill in this regard, to the point that, much like Yoda{{Film|II}} or [[Revan/Legends|Revan]],''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' he could block Sith lightning with his hands. As a Sith he also used [[Force speed/Legends|Force speed]] and could [[Force jump/Legends|leap great distances]] with the aid of the energy field that binds the universe together.{{Film|IV}} His attacks sometimes struck more people when boosted with the Force. He knew the technique of [[Battlemind|Battlestrike]]. Krayt had a talent for [[Farsight/Legends|seeing those he knew of]] or sought after from afar, as well as [[Force vision/Legends|predicting the future]] as he was able to foresee that he would be reborn and able to heal himself of his Yuuzhan Vong implants and when he predicted the Galactic Alliance's and Fel Empire's tactics during the Battle of Tavias before they had even come up with the plans themsevles. He seemed to have a "recorder on the eye of fate", according to [[Darth Wyyrlok (I)|Darth Wyyrlock]]. The Force spoke to Darth Krayt, guiding him towards victory such that he knew the war-plans of his enemies before they had even devised them. Additionally, Krayt was able to survive falling off a cliff, despite being roasted with lightning from a nearly Sidious-like opponent working in harmony with his Jedi Master host, and Krayt being impaled with a lightsaber shortly beforehand, by using the Force, to [[Force barrier/Legends|soften]] his landing. All the more noteworthy, this is, since Grandmaster Yoda strained to achieve similar results when he (tired after nearly defeating the Sith) departed Sidious.[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (junior novelization)|''Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith'' {{C|junior novelization}}]] Darth Krayt also mastered and even improved ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith techniques]] that prolong life, allowing him to survive for more than a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] despite his failing body when combined with his use of [[Hibernation trance/Legends|Force Trance]], though he was partially sustained through stasis. His knowledge of Sith magic also was enough for him to craft a Holocron, which required a mastery of sorcery and esoteric glyphs. He thought he could form [[Force-bond/Legends|bonds]] with others given time and moral agreement. Krayt resisted the tricks of Andeddu. He may have slowed down the flow of time for himself. He also demonstrated a certain degree of resistance towards Mind Tricks, such as when he tricked Darth Wyyrlok into believing he was being affected by [[Memory Walk|his mental powers]]. Wyyrlock III, it should be noted, had previously demonstrated his mental prowess when he destroyed ancient the Sith Lord [[Darth Andeddu]] in a battle of wills and illusions and used his powers to make the members of the [[Malevolence (cult)|Malevolence cult]] slay each other. Krayt also had a very acute [[Force sense/Legends|Force sense]], demonstrated when he sensed Cade Skywalker healing his Master during the Massacre of Ossus half-way across the galaxy, sensed Cade on the same planet (where millions lived) detected the emotional state of Cade Skywalker, and when he sensed the presence of a spy within the Imperial ranks. As a Padawan, however, he had not yet developed such skill and Masters Mundi and Gallia able to sense the future and the guidance of the Force better than he could, however he was able to sense impending danger and darkness as well as the Masters.[[Star Wars (1998) 30|''Star Wars'' (1998) 30]] He also, from across the galaxy, sensed the intent of Vader and the 501 coming to purge the Jedi Temple and the death-pains of the younglings as though he had been there. The Emperor also had developed the rare ability to detect the Yuzzhan Vong with the Force, much like Jacen Solo or Luke Skywalker could.''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' As Hett, he was able to [[Sense Force Potential|detect Force sensitivity]] in others. Darth Krayt was noted to master of the [[Alter Environment]] skill and able to control the weather of Korriban, such as by causing massive storms and flashes of lightning in the sky, allegedly this was done while he was in stasis.[[Riptide (novel)|''Riptide'']] Unlike other Sith Lords such as [[Tenebrae]], Krayt did not require the use of rituals to bend the weather.{{TORcite|type=codex|Galactic History 31: The Ritual of Nathema}}[[Riptide (novel)|''Riptide'']] The leader of the Sith could [[Force stealth|hide his presence]] in the Force, even from other Sith and the Jedi Grandmaster, though when in the same room Luke Skywalker could sense his dark side affiliations, to the concern of the Jedi. Ben Skywalker and [[Vestara Khai]], even with the help of her hounds, could not find Krayt when they were on Korriban. Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade Skywalker were unable to use the Force to learn anything about him, other than his species and hints of his appearance, with the Grandmaster of the Jedi being baffled as to how "the Dark Man" seemed to not exist in the future, yet also loom as a threat. After he returned from death, the Dreadlord was able to also conceal his allies from the Force sense of others, and his return and signal to his followers went unnoticed by the Jedi, save for Cade Skywalker, who had been among those contacted. Despite the titanic exertion of dark side power, the New Jedi Order’s Jedi Council was completely unable to sense his use of the Force across the galaxy or even his resurrection. Darth Krayt was also adept in the use of [[Mind Walking]], allowing him to travel [[beyond shadows]], such as when he helped Luke Skywalker defeat Abeloth. He was also proficient in the use of [[Force drain/Legends|Force drain]], which he used in his battle against Abeloth--plundering the entity's [[Personal Force reservoir|Force energy]] while harming her and Luke Skywalker, though at great cost to himself. The damage done to her reserves of power was such that formerly mighty [[Sith shadow-ghoul]]s Abeloth maintained on Coruscant were left almost impotent. Krayt's drain was also able to pull away Luke Skywalker's health and might since he was too close to Abeloth, prompting the Grandmaster to threaten to free the Bringer of Chaos if the Dark Lord did not relent his attack on all three of them. Krayt could employ [[Force deflection/Legends|Force Deflection]], at least against Force Powers, The Sith Lord could [[Consume Essence|feed on the suffering of others]], even to the point of briefly counteracting most of the physical detriments he suffered when his ordered massacres on the homeworld of the Mon Cala. Similarly, he could use his own anger for greater power.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' He was completely unaffected by Cade Skywalker's [[Force scream/Legends|Force Scream]], without needing to offer any overt defense. This showing that he have resistence toward fear-based force techniques. Krayt would use the Force to improve his skills of deceiving and noticing things, and, with great effort, he could use the Force against anything he saw within the same solar system. He was also able to swiftly replenish his [[personal Force reservoir]]. Hett also had an affinity with the use of [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]], such that hold verbal telepathic conversations with those nearby, such as when he communed with the Jedi [[Xiaan Amersu|Xiaan]] and encouraged her to try to survive, though ultimately she chose to give her life to bring the mission success, and Hett held such a conversation when both of them were dogfighting in their starships.[[Republic 77|''Republic'' 77]] Per to the standards of [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Master Fisto]], a Jedi being able to do things like this was a mark of great power in the Force.{{CWACite|1|Fierce Currents}} When Order 66 was carried out, he, like Grandmaster Yoda, sensed the echoes of so many Jedi dying, even feeling the echoes of the younglings that Vader slew. As the Dark Lord, he made the use of telepathy even more often and dramatically, such that he would keep in mental contact with his most trusted servants at all times, regardless of which planet they were on, and his skill was shown again when, shortly after returning himself from beyond death, he communicated with Darth Talon to release him from stasis on Korriban just after he resurrected himself. Almost a century before, much like his deeds in the Clone Wars, Krayt was able to mentally send whole sentences to Luke Skywalker, regardless of if the Grandmaster wished this contact. Likewise, he forced Wryyylok III to listen to a mental description of his life's story and philosophy just before killing the rebel. He could tempt others with the dark side. He also could read the minds of his followers while his body was in stasis. Darth Talon credited Krayt with being able to [[Drain Knowledge|tear knowledge out of the mind]] of Marasiah Fel.[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]] Krayt could detect lies. Krayt could [[Mind trick/Legends|trick and confuse others with the Force]] and as such could cause mental strain to opponents so that they would pass out. After he resurrected himself, he sent out a [[Force call|signal to all Sith in the entire galaxy]] (as well as two non-Sith who were tempted by the Dark Side), alerting them to his return from death and putting them on notice for his imminent reclaiming of authority. As part of this surge through the Force, he [[Enhance Force Sensitivity|empowered]] ''all'' of the Sith in the galaxy, save for the traitorous [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Wryyylok]]. Krayt was stated to have the power to [[Battle meditation/Legends|command]] millions of soldiers with his will, [[Qâzoi Kyantuska|mind-controlling]] armies of [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith Troopers]]. He could invade the minds of others, making it hard for them to use the Force at their full capabilities without being tainted by the dark side of the Force. Darth Krayt was so skilled that he projected an illusion of Cade's possible future into the young Skywalker's mind, which shook Cade a great deal. Later after subduing, killing, and resurrecting him, Krayt forced Cade to view a future of his own design--a realm of fire, death, rebirth, and the ascendency of the dark side of the Force. Before turning to the dark side, A'Sharad Hett was trained to use [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], though the extent of his healing abilities is unknown. As a Jedi Knight, he would have been expected to be capable of using [[Curato salva]]. When Abeloth cut off his hand beyond shadows, a wound that would have normally transferred over to his physical form, he reclaimed and restored the hand. As a point of comparison, Luke Skywalker was left with lasting wounds from smilar blows.[[Crucible (novel)|''Crucible'']] For a long time, the Dragon of the Sith was unable to overcome the burdens of his armor and age, try as he might to use the dark to endure. However, after the Battle of Had Abbadon, Darth Krayt was finally able to completely heal himself of his Yuuzhan Vong growths as well as what would normally be severe mortal wounds, demonstrating that his skill in Force Healing had greatly increased, and also worthy of note due to the fact that the Yuuzhan Vong were almost impossible to touch with the Force, save with lightning. While before the battle his power over death''[[Star Wars: Beware the Sith]]'' had been limited to resisting aging, now he was able to wield the Force to resurrect his own corpse post-mortem. During his final duel with Cade Skywalker, Krayt revealed that when he experienced death on Had Abbadon, he discovered the rare dark side power of [[dark transfer]], allowing him to return from death itself and explosively augment his already considerable healing powers--even to the point of restoring his withered flesh to a fit and strong state. Darth Krayt could also use Dark Transfer in tandem with Force Lightning to injure or kill such as when he killed and resurrected Cade Skywalker during their final duel, much like how Cade himself used his Dark Transfer. From beyond the grave, the Sith Lord taunted Cade Skywalker that his proficiency with Dark Transfer advanced to the degree in which he would heal himself even from [[Death/Legends|death]] itself, according to the testimony of Cade Skywalker. In addition to learning Dark Transfer from his death experience, Krayt revealed that he could see [[Shatterpoint/Legends|shatterpoints]] in the world around him and chain the use of that power to augment his Dark Side healing or damaging powers. After his final death, Krayt's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] claimed that he learned the dangerous''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' art of [[Transfer essence|essence transfer]] and threatened to return to life within Cade Skywalker if his body was destroyed. Despite this boasts, Krayt's spirit ceased to trouble Skywalker's mind after his body was burned. ===Other skills=== A'Sharad Hett was a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] and peacemaker of some skill, as shown when he was able to calm Anakin Skywalker and convince him to admit his killing of a Tuskan tribe. He became a skilled leader to the point he was made a Jedi Master who was entrusted with the training of a Padawan and also general during the Clone Wars and then commanded Tuskan Raider tribes until his defeat and mutilation at the hand of Kenobi. He was skilled at the leadership of Tuskans and Sith and could inspire them to fight harder. Some Tuskans were so inspired by him that they went off-world to try to find and serve him. Later, he founded an entire Sith order. It was Krayt, not Caedus or the Lost Tribe, that truly restored the Sith tradition and legacy to the galaxy after the defeat of Darth Sidious, even to the point of making things the most grim and dark they had ever been in the galaxy, with the possible exception of Sidious's reign as Emperor. Krayt also was able to focus the rage of his Sith, nearly negating their perpensity for infighting. As a Sith he used [[Dun Möch/Legends|taunts and insults]] in battle. He used a [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|historical example]] to guide him in trying to corrupt Cade Skywalker. A'Sharad Hett was well learned in the study of Mon Calamari and ceremonial starships. Hett was able to become a bounty hunter and avoided detection as a Jedi by the Empire. He knew the [[Code of the Sith/Legends|Sith Code]]. A'Sharad Hett was also a skilled pilot as proven during the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battles of Geonosis]] and [[Battle of Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]]. His skills as a pilot were such that he could navigate a Yuuzhan Vong coralskipper even though he had no prior knowledge of how to operate it. As Krayt, he also seemed to have a considerable knowledge of engineering and robotics since he was able to start his Sith Trooper Project, in addition to the construction of the Annihilator starfighters, while keeping it hidden for decades from the other Sith. He also could make Lightsabers. Darth Krayt was able to create plans that stretched for hundreds of years, at least according to his servants. Darth Krayt was a great [[Philosopher/Legends|philosopher]] and thinker, able to understand the flaws of the various Sith and government systems, and created a very loyal Sith Order and by force was able to establish an empire that might well have been the greatest of all Sith achievements. Of course, not all agreed with his ideals, such as the Fels who viewed him as nothing more than a thief. Krayt's war was considered by him to have broken the spine of the galaxy. He could remember well all the events he had lived through. Through unknown means, Krayt was in control of the One Sith in 140 ABY.''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'' Hett was swift enough to block the Jedi Master Mundi from unlawfully helping a man up, when he was only fifteen years old. In a moment he was able to rush and grapple Aura Sing before she could bring a lightsaber down on his father.''[[Star Wars (1998) 12|Republic 12]]'' He was able to survive being hit with a barrage of Thud Bugs and being bitten by an Amphistaff, though he was wearing armor at the time. Darth Krayt came out less hurt than Skywalker after their battle with Abeloth, even after having taken the brunt of a massive bolt of lightning, resisting a Force blast from Abeloth, having to subject himself to his own Force Drain to catch Abeloth, and having his hand cut off in that realm, much like how he was better off when he came to blows with the Grandmaster's [[Parent/Legends|father]]. Moreover, he was by unknown means able to get close enough to her homeworld to use Mind Walking in his hunt for Abeloth. Such was Krayt's power that Luke Skywalker was afraid of him. While beyond shadows Krayt was able to latch on to Abeloth and began to rip her form apart with his hand. Krayt was a cunning master of [[Ground warfare/Legends|warfare]].[[Star Wars Insider 88|''Star Wars Insider'' 88]] Hett could lift a [[Yaddle/Legends|member]] of [[Yoda's species/Legends|Yoda's species]]. Krayt could lift human males off the ground with one arm.[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]] Krayt was able to find many Sith relics. He was competent in the use of Yuuzhan Vong armor. While in this armor, he survived being shot with heavy weapons, blasted with dark lightning, conjured by a Sith of nearly-Sidious's power working with a Jedi Master, the Force Storm so powerful that it illuminated his skeleton, and impaled with a lightsaber, before being thrown off a cliff. Hett was skilled in a number of languages, such as Galactic Basic Standard, [[Tusken/Legends|Tusken]], and [[Bocce/Legends|Bocce]]. While studying the ways of the Sith, he had learned to speak the [[Sith (language)|ancient tongue]]. He and Darth Wyyrlok III were the only known members of the One Sith to possess the language. Hett had a keen sense of smell, such that he could detect other biomes while within a city.[[Star Wars (1998) 14|''Star Wars'' (1998) 14]] As a Sith, Krayt was immune to the effects of Omega Red. ==Lightsabers== File:ASharadHettLightsaber-CSWE.jpg|A'Sharad Hett's original Jedi lightsaber. File:Sharad_Hett_saber.jpg|Sharad Hett's weapon, inherited by A'Sharad. File:Krayt_saber.jpg|One of Darth Krayt's twin Sith lightsabers. Krayt wielded several lightsabers during his lifetime. As A'Sharad Hett, his first weapon was constructed during his days as a Tusken Raider, while receiving informal Jedi training from his father. This weapon, like his father's, possessed a red blade, though the source of the [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|focusing crystal]] used in it remains unknown. A'Sharad inherited the weapon of his father after Sharad's death. Rather than favoring one weapon or the other, Hett took to wielding both at the same time, becoming an exceptionally talented [[Jar'Kai/Legends|dual-blade duelist]].[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] He later replaced the red crystals in both his and his father's weapon with lime green ones. When he resurfaced as Darth Krayt, Hett had abandoned the use of his original Jedi sabers, instead he now wielded a pair of Sith lightsabers. While the specifications of the internal mechanism remains unknown, the casings of the twin weapons were composed of a type of coral substance clearly inspired by Yuuzhan Vong biotechnology. The [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|red-bladed]] weapons were stored in a cross formation on his back when not in use. After Krayt's rebirth, he continued to use one of his Sith lightsabers, though he again took up his original Jedi lightsaber, and after killing Wyyrlok had taken out the green crystal and replaced it with a synthetic red crystal, giving it the bloodshine blade of a Sith lightsaber. ==Behind the scenes== The character of A'Sharad Hett was developed by author [[Timothy Truman]] and debuted in the [[Star Wars: Outlander|''Outlander'']] [[Dark Horse Comics]] comic arc. A'Sharad's face was not seen until [[Republic 59|''Enemy Lines'']], almost five years after the character's debut. In the back of [[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]], [[Randy Stradley]] made known that Krayt's identity would be revealed in [[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]]. Furthermore, in the back of [[Legacy (2006) 11|''Legacy'' (2006) 11]], Stradley hinted that it might be someone the fans already know or will soon know before ''Legacy 15'' but encouraged readers to be patient until the issue was released. Before Krayt's identity was revealed in [[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]], there was already speculation that Krayt and A'Sharad Hett were one and the same. Mostly this is due to their practice of Jar'Kai, their similar facial tattoos, and Krayt being named after the krayt dragon of Tatooine, Hett's homeworld. [[File:Darth_Krayt_Battle_of_the_Sith_Lords.png|thumb|right|180px|Concept art of Darth Krayt's redesigned appearance in ''Battle of the Sith Lords'']] When the revelation was made, editor Randy Stradley commented at the end of the issue, "''I know. You're all excited because you finally know who Darth Krayt really is. Sure, sure, some of you even guessed. Give yourself a pat on the back. Still, you have to admit, it was quite a reveal''". Another common fan theory at that time was that Krayt was going to be revealed as Jacen Solo at some point of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' book series, but with the announcement of the "[[Darth Who Contest]]" and the selection of the name Darth Caedus for Solo, this theory was proved wrong. The depiction of A'Sharad Hett in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] bears a resemblance to an [[Official Pix]] promotional image of Anakin Skywalker.[[:File:Anakin2sabers.jpg|Image of Anakin Skywalker]] In [[Legacy (2006) 50|''Legacy'' (2006) 50]], Krayt is depicted fighting Cade while donning a [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric]] in a vision. This truned out to not be the true future, misleading or wrong as visions often are,''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' as seen in [[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy—War'' 6]] which shows Krayt donning combat armor. Darth Krayt was to appear with a new design as the main foe in [[Red Fly Studio]]'s ''[[Battle of the Sith Lords]]'', coming into conflict with [[Maul's descendant]] and Darth Talon, who would have pitted against him for violating the Rule of Two.{{WebCite|url=https://www.gameinformer.com/b/features/archive/2014/04/09/art-from-the-darth-maul-game-you-39-ll-never-get-to-play.aspx|text=Art From
